title: Federal Information Processing Standard

infobox.value: (**FIPS**) of the United States are a set of publicly announced [standards](/wiki/Standardization "Standardization") that the [National Institute of Standards and Technology](/wiki/National_Institute_of_Standards_and_Technology "National Institute of Standards and Technology") (NIST) has developed for use in computer systems of non-military United States government agencies and contractors.[[1]](#cite_note-GenInfo-1) FIPS standards establish requirements for ensuring computer security and interoperability, and are intended for cases in which suitable industry standards do not already exist.[[1]](#cite_note-GenInfo-1) Many FIPS specifications are modified versions of standards the technical communities use, such as the [American National Standards Institute](/wiki/American_National_Standards_Institute "American National Standards Institute") (ANSI), the [Institute of Electrical and Electronics Engineers](/wiki/Institute_of_Electrical_and_Electronics_Engineers "Institute of Electrical and Electronics Engineers") (IEEE), and the [International Organization for Standardization](/wiki/International_Organization_for_Standardization "International Organization for Standardization") (ISO).

["Minimum Security Requirements for Federal Information and Information Systems"](https://web.archive.org/web/20060509224952/http://csrc.nist.gov/publications/fips/fips200/FIPS-200-final-march.pdf) (PDF). 2007-03-01. Archived from [the original](http://csrc.nist.gov/publications/fips/fips200/FIPS-200-final-march.pdf) (PDF) on 2006-05-09. Retrieved 2015-04-01.

["87-20-20 Key Escrow Encryption Policies and Technologies"](https://web.archive.org/web/20150214152130/http://www.ittoday.info/AIMS/DSM/87-20-20.pdf) (PDF). 1998-06-01. Archived from [the original](http://www.ittoday.info/AIMS/DSM/87-20-20.pdf) (PDF) on 2015-02-14. Retrieved 2015-02-14.

Turner, James M. (2008-09-02). ["Announcing Approval of the Withdrawal of Ten Federal Information Processing Standards (FIPS)"](http://frwebgate.access.gpo.gov/cgi-bin/getpage.cgi?position=all&page=51276&dbname=2008_register). *[NIST](/wiki/National_Institute_of_Standards_and_Technology "National Institute of Standards and Technology")*. **73**. [Federal Register](/wiki/Federal_Register "Federal Register"): 51276. Retrieved 2017-11-02.

From Wikipedia, the free encyclopedia

(Redirected from [Federal Information Processing Standard](/w/index.php?title=Federal_Information_Processing_Standard&redirect=no "Federal Information Processing Standard"))

Technical standard

The **Federal Information Processing Standards** (**FIPS**) of the United States are a set of publicly announced [standards](/wiki/Standardization "Standardization") that the [National Institute of Standards and Technology](/wiki/National_Institute_of_Standards_and_Technology "National Institute of Standards and Technology") (NIST) has developed for use in computer systems of non-military United States government agencies and contractors.[[1]](#cite_note-GenInfo-1) FIPS standards establish requirements for ensuring computer security and interoperability, and are intended for cases in which suitable industry standards do not already exist.[[1]](#cite_note-GenInfo-1) Many FIPS specifications are modified versions of standards the technical communities use, such as the [American National Standards Institute](/wiki/American_National_Standards_Institute "American National Standards Institute") (ANSI), the [Institute of Electrical and Electronics Engineers](/wiki/Institute_of_Electrical_and_Electronics_Engineers "Institute of Electrical and Electronics Engineers") (IEEE), and the [International Organization for Standardization](/wiki/International_Organization_for_Standardization "International Organization for Standardization") (ISO).

## Specific areas of FIPS standardization

[[edit](/w/index.php?title=Federal_Information_Processing_Standards&action=edit&section=1 "Edit section: Specific areas of FIPS standardization")]

The U.S. government has developed various FIPS specifications to standardize a number of topics including:

* Codes, e.g., [FIPS county codes](/wiki/FIPS_county_code "FIPS county code") or codes to indicate weather conditions or emergency indications. In 1994, [National Oceanic and Atmospheric Administration](/wiki/National_Oceanic_and_Atmospheric_Administration "National Oceanic and Atmospheric Administration") (NOAA) began broadcasting FIPS codes along with their standard weather broadcasts from local stations. These codes identify the type of emergency and the specific geographic area, such as a county, affected by the emergency.
* Encryption standards, such as [AES](/wiki/Advanced_Encryption_Standard "Advanced Encryption Standard") (FIPS 197),[[2]](#cite_note-2) and its predecessor, the withdrawn [56-bit DES](/wiki/Data_Encryption_Standard "Data Encryption Standard") (FIPS 46-3).[[3]](#cite_note-3)

## Data security standards

[[edit](/w/index.php?title=Federal_Information_Processing_Standards&action=edit&section=2 "Edit section: Data security standards")]

Some FIPS standards are related to the security of data processing systems.[[4]](#cite_note-4) Some of these include the use of [key escrow](/wiki/Key_escrow "Key escrow") systems.[[5]](#cite_note-5)[[6]](#cite_note-6)

## Withdrawal of geographic codes

[[edit](/w/index.php?title=Federal_Information_Processing_Standards&action=edit&section=3 "Edit section: Withdrawal of geographic codes")]

Some examples of FIPS Codes for geographical areas include FIPS 10-4 for [country codes](/wiki/List_of_FIPS_country_codes "List of FIPS country codes") or [region codes](/wiki/List_of_FIPS_region_codes "List of FIPS region codes") and FIPS 5-2 for [state codes](/wiki/FIPS_state_code "FIPS state code"). These codes were similar to or comparable with, but not the same as, [ISO 3166](/wiki/ISO_3166 "ISO 3166"), or the [NUTS](/wiki/Nomenclature_of_Territorial_Units_for_Statistics "Nomenclature of Territorial Units for Statistics") standard of the [European Union](/wiki/European_Union "European Union"). In 2002, the [National Institute of Standards and Technology](/wiki/National_Institute_of_Standards_and_Technology "National Institute of Standards and Technology") (NIST) withdrew several geographic FIPS code standards, including those for countries (FIPS 10-4), [U.S. states](/wiki/U.S._state "U.S. state") (FIPS 5-2), and [counties](/wiki/County_(United_States) "County (United States)") ([FIPS 6-4](/wiki/FIPS_county_code "FIPS county code")).[[7]](#cite_note-withdraw-7)[[8]](#cite_note-fr-8) These are to be replaced by ISO 3166 and [INCITS](/wiki/International_Committee_for_Information_Technology_Standards "International Committee for Information Technology Standards") standards 38 and 31, respectively.[[9]](#cite_note-replacement_chart-9) Some of the codes maintain the previous numerical system, particularly for states.[[10]](#cite_note-ansi-10)

In 2008, NIST withdrew the FIPS 55-3 database.[[7]](#cite_note-withdraw-7) This database included 5-digit numeric place codes for cities, towns, and villages, or other centers of population in the United States. The codes were assigned alphabetically to places within each state, and as a result changed frequently in order to maintain the alphabetical sorting. NIST replaced these codes with the more permanent [GNIS](/wiki/Geographic_Names_Information_System "Geographic Names Information System") Feature ID, maintained by the [U.S. Board on Geographic Names](/wiki/U.S._Board_on_Geographic_Names "U.S. Board on Geographic Names"). The GNIS database is the official geographic names repository database for the United States, and is designated the only source of geographic names and locative attributes for use by the agencies of the Federal Government.[[11]](#cite_note-11) FIPS 8-6 "Metropolitan Areas" and 9-1 "Congressional Districts of the U.S." were also withdrawn in 2008, to be replaced with [INCITS](/wiki/International_Committee_for_Information_Technology_Standards "International Committee for Information Technology Standards") standards 454 and 455, respectively.[[9]](#cite_note-replacement_chart-9)

The [U.S. Census Bureau](/wiki/U.S._Census_Bureau "U.S. Census Bureau") used FIPS place codes database to identify legal and statistical entities for county subdivisions, places, and [American Indian](/wiki/Indigenous_peoples_of_the_Americas "Indigenous peoples of the Americas") areas, [Alaska Native](/wiki/Alaska_Natives "Alaska Natives") areas, or [Hawaiian home lands](/wiki/Native_Hawaiians "Native Hawaiians") when they needed to present census data for these areas.[[12]](#cite_note-12)

In response to the NIST decision, the Census Bureau is in the process of transitioning over to the GNIS Feature ID, which will be completed after the [2010 census](/wiki/2010_United_States_census "2010 United States census").[*[needs update](/wiki/Wikipedia:Manual_of_Style/Dates_and_numbers#Chronological_items "Wikipedia:Manual of Style/Dates and numbers")*] Until then, previously issued FIPS place codes, renamed "Census Code", will continue to be used, with the Census bureau assigning new codes as needed for their internal use during the transition.[[10]](#cite_note-ansi-10)[[13]](#cite_note-transition-13)

## See also

[[edit](/w/index.php?title=Federal_Information_Processing_Standards&action=edit&section=4 "Edit section: See also")]

* [Federal Information Security Management Act of 2002](/wiki/Federal_Information_Security_Management_Act_of_2002 "Federal Information Security Management Act of 2002") (FISMA)
* [FIPS 137](/wiki/FIPS_137 "FIPS 137") (Federal Standard for [Linear Predictive Coding](/wiki/Linear_predictive_coding "Linear predictive coding"))
* [FIPS 140](/wiki/FIPS_140 "FIPS 140") (*Security requirements for [cryptography](/wiki/Cryptographic "Cryptographic") modules*)
* [FIPS 153](/wiki/FIPS_153 "FIPS 153") (3D graphics)
* [FIPS 197](/wiki/FIPS_197 "FIPS 197") ([Rijndael](/wiki/Rijndael "Rijndael") / [AES](/wiki/Advanced_Encryption_Standard "Advanced Encryption Standard") cipher)
* [FIPS 199](/wiki/FIPS_199 "FIPS 199") (*Standards for Security Categorization of Federal Information and Information Systems*)
* [FIPS 201](/wiki/FIPS_201 "FIPS 201") (*Personal Identity Verification for Federal Employees and Contractors*)
* [List of FIPS region codes](/wiki/List_of_FIPS_region_codes "List of FIPS region codes")
* [List of FIPS state codes](/wiki/Federal_Information_Processing_Standard_state_code "Federal Information Processing Standard state code")
